mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 6.2% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 6.2% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO decline are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening economy that increases corporate profitability and reduces risk premia, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 increase are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the housing market index above 1000 signals a strengthening housing market that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP and a 3-month increase in the housing market index above 1000 signals a strengthening economy and housing market that increases corporate profitability and reduces risk premia, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both GDPC1 increase and HOUST > 1000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 3-month increase in real GDP signals a weakening monetary policy and strengthening economy that reduces corporate profitability and increases risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both FEDFUNDS decline and GDPC1 increase are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production and a 3-month decline in the housing market index below 1000 signals a weakening supply chain and housing market that reduces corporate profitability and increases risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both INDPRO decline and HOUST < 1000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.
